data_out.description: Docker image successfully pulled and container launched; tima entry point responds to help or version command with non-error output

domain_knowledge: TIMA is a taxonomically informed metabolite annotation tool with an R-based implementation distributed as a Docker container.
The adafede/tima-r Docker image is the canonical containerized distribution of the TIMA software from the taxonomicallyinformedannotation GitHub organization.
Container entry points must be tested to confirm they are properly configured and callable within the running container environment.

direct_checks: verify Docker image adafede/tima-r can be pulled from hub.docker.com without authentication errors
verify container launches without crashing (exit code 0 or expected interactive state within 30 seconds)
verify tima entry point is callable within running container (e.g., 'tima --help' or 'tima --version' returns output with exit code 0)
verify help or version output contains recognizable tima identifier or version string
expert_review: confirm that the entry point command and output format align with documented tima R package API expectations
assess whether any startup warnings or deprecations in container logs indicate functional issues

methodology_summary: Retrieve the TIMA Docker image (adafede/tima-r) from Docker Hub.
Invoke the tima entry point with a help or version flag to confirm availability and correct operation.
Validation: Verify that the container starts without errors and the tima help/version command exits with status code 0 and produces non-empty output.

task_description: Pull the TIMA Docker image from adafede/tima-r and verify container startup and availability of the tima entry point via a help or version command.
task_objective: Pull the tima Docker image from hub.docker.com/r/adafede/tima-r and verify the container launches and responds to a help or version command.

workflow_description: 1. Pull the Docker image adafede/tima-r from Docker Hub using docker pull. 2. Launch a container instance from the pulled image. 3. Execute a tima help or version command within the running container to confirm the entry point is accessible and functional.
